trigger: User requested to start the self-improvement workflow after activation.
problem: Memory artifacts can drift when bootstrap, pattern extraction, iteration logging, and context updates are not run together.
root_cause: The skill was available, but this session had no fresh end-to-end workflow execution record.
solution: Executed the full workflow chain and synchronized patterns, iterations, context, and decision records in one pass.
reusable_pattern: Treat self-improvement runs as atomic: bootstrap -> patterns -> iteration -> context/decisions.

trigger: User requested activation of a self-improvement agent skill.
problem: No standalone self-improvement skill was installed or discoverable in the active Codex skills directory.
root_cause: The workflow existed as separate base skills, but no composed skill package was present.
solution: Composed the workflow from 5 base skills and generated an installable self-improvement-agent skill.
failed_approaches: Search only curated remote skill list
Search only current repo worktree for SKILL.md
reusable_pattern: If a meta-skill is missing, chain existing base skills and generate a composite installable skill.

trigger: User requested self-improvement and autonomous work on the next open repository priority.
problem: Several FastAPI route handlers were declared async but still used blocking time.sleep-based release and polling loops, which could stall the event loop during camera calibration and pipeline start/stop transitions.
root_cause: Lifecycle waits had grown incrementally around Windows camera release timing and startup polling without a shared non-blocking helper for async routes.
solution: Introduced centralized async wait helpers in src.web.routes, replaced the blocking sleeps with await asyncio.sleep-based polling, and added regression tests for single/multi start-stop plus stereo calibration waits.
failed_approaches: Monkeypatched the shared threading.Thread module in route tests, which leaked into the FastAPI lifespan startup path and corrupted thread-handle state.
reusable_pattern: Centralize non-blocking wait/poll helpers in async FastAPI routes; avoid time.sleep and, in TestClient-based tests, patch the worker target rather than the shared threading module.

trigger: Continuation on the next open runtime hardening priority after P19.
problem: Pending hits depended on frontend timeout logic and had no authoritative server-side cap or expiry policy, risking stale state and unbounded growth under long sessions.
root_cause: Candidate lifecycle concerns were split across pipeline callbacks, routes, and the UI without a single backend owner for insert, pop, expire, and metrics.
solution: Added centralized pending-hit helpers in src.main, enforced TTL and overflow trimming, ran periodic cleanup in the pipeline loops, and exposed lifecycle counters in /api/stats.
failed_approaches: Initial overflow test data used timestamps that triggered TTL cleanup first, so the test hit the wrong behavior.
reusable_pattern: For shared runtime queues, centralize add/pop/expire/clear in one backend module, enforce TTL and max-size at insertion time, and expose lifecycle counters so routes and UI consume a single authoritative view.

trigger: Continuation on the next runtime hardening priority after server-side pending-hit lifecycle work.
problem: Pipeline references, thread handles, and multi-frame caches in app_state were mutated from several locations without a clear contract, and multiple tests relied on pre-lifespan global state that real app startup now resets.
root_cause: Lifecycle state had grown as open dict writes across main.py, routes.py, and tests, while FastAPI lifespan was not treated as the authoritative runtime reset boundary.
solution: Added src.utils.state as the shared owner for runtime-state initialization and lifecycle mutations, switched main.py and routes.py to these helpers, and moved lifespan-sensitive test setup inside TestClient startup.
failed_approaches: Repaired only route tests first; a second pass was needed after readiness tests exposed the same pre-lifespan setup assumption.
reusable_pattern: When startup becomes deterministic, centralize shared runtime-state mutation behind helpers and set mocked state only after TestClient has entered the FastAPI lifespan.

trigger: Continuation on the next open priority after P23 with calibration still identified as the weakest maintainability area.
problem: src/cv/calibration.py still bundled board calibration math, YAML persistence, constants and repeated ChArUco observation logic, which kept the module hard to reason about and left persistence/error paths under-tested.
root_cause: Calibration logic grew around a single legacy manager while newer calibration managers and tests still depended on that same monolithic implementation seam.
solution: Split board workflows, config store/defaults and shared ChArUco observation collection into dedicated helper modules while keeping CalibrationManager as the stable public facade, and added persistence and error-path tests.
reusable_pattern: When a calibration manager becomes monolithic, split pure workflow helpers and shared IO/detection helpers first, then keep the manager facade stable so routes and dependent managers do not need to change.

trigger: Continuation on the next runtime priority after calibration hardening, with multi-camera burst handling still identified as an open risk.
problem: Multi-camera fusion stored only the latest detection per camera, so short burst sequences could overwrite earlier hits before fusion and make timeout behavior non-deterministic.
root_cause: The fusion buffer modeled one slot per camera and cleared entire camera state after timeout/fusion, instead of preserving ordered short-lived detections until they were actually consumed.
solution: Changed the fusion buffer to a small per-camera time window, matched around the oldest pending anchor, removed only consumed entries, and extended tests for burst ordering and timeout preservation.
failed_approaches: Initial retention window was too short and pruned unmatched detections before they could emit as timeout fallbacks.
reusable_pattern: For burst-sensitive multi-camera fusion, buffer a short ordered window per camera, pair around the oldest pending anchor, and remove only the detections actually consumed by timeout or fusion.

trigger: Continuation after runtime priorities, with a remaining backlog item to clean historically tracked generated artifacts from the worktree.
problem: Generated __pycache__ and related Python cache artifacts still appeared in git status even though ignore rules already existed.
root_cause: The cache paths had been committed earlier, so .gitignore no longer affected them while they remained in the Git index.
solution: Removed the tracked cache directories from the Git index with git rm --cached -r, kept the ignore rules in place, and documented the hygiene step in the development workflow.
reusable_pattern: If generated Python artifacts are already tracked, untrack them from Git with git rm --cached and document the hygiene step; ignore rules alone will not clean the worktree.
